emails.subject: Re: MHI 501G
emails.body: Re: MHI 501G We are still looking for capital costs for a 730 MW plant in Texas, which comprises of 4 FA's and 2 STG's. We are currently using $450 MM for a total project cost number, which comes out to be $616/Kw. Thanks Ben

emails.body: Re: MHI 501G We are still looking for capital costs for a 730 MW plant in Texas, which comprises of 4 FA's and 2 STG's. We are currently using $450 MM for a total project cost number, which comes out to be $616/Kw. Thanks Ben

emails.subject: Re: MHI 501G
emails.body: Maybe the message is garbled but 4 7FA's and 2 STG's (probably two 2 X 1 CCGT's) should be nominally $470,000,000 based on the Pittsburg CCGT out in California (but includes no real EPC contractor margin/contingency so will not match what Nepco or other third parties might bid), would nominally be 480 MW at 95F for each 2 X 1 so about 960 MW for all. I think this works out to about $489/kW. This based on about $34,000,000 for each GE7FA so pricing will adjust if market price higher or lower. I honestly don't and have not seen a 501G price. I did see 701G prices in Asia and they appeared to be about 5% lower per kilowatt than 701F and GE9FA's. In this hot market they maybe at a premium due to better heat rate and larger unit size. I would suggest $515/kW as a go by. I have called John Garrison to see what current pricing and delivery is also to see if performance might be a touch better than our maybe two year old model. Do we want to get an estimate from Nepco or a benchmark from my estimator's? I would need a charge number/tasking letter. Bruce Benjamin Rogers@ECT 05/04/2000 11:24 AM

emails.subject: Re: MHI 501G
emails.body: Ben This sure sounds like a deal Len Gunderson was looking at early this year and a large chemical facility. The capacity for the project did not match what equipment it was based on any more details so I can see if we have any data from that earlier look? Bruce Benjamin Rogers@ECT 05/04/2000 11:24 AM
